plugin.name: ninjaslash
plugin._long: Close animation that slices the window in two along a fast sword stroke. A glowing blade sweeps across, then the two halves separate perpendicular to the cut, spin, fall with gravity and fade off the screen edges. Inspired by Fruit Ninja. Register it as a close animation with [animate] close_animation = ninjaslash.

option.name: duration
option._long: How long the whole slash lasts, from the blade sweep to the halves leaving the screen.

option.name: pattern
option._long: How the window is cut. "random" (default) picks a fresh pattern on every close. "parallel" makes one or more parallel cuts (see Slices) that sweep in order. "cross" makes two perpendicular strokes, cutting the window into four pieces that fly to the corners (looks like an X when the angle is 45).

option.name: cuts
option._long: Parallel pattern only: how many sword strokes cut the window, each sweeping in order. 1 is the classic single diagonal slash (two halves); N cuts leave N+1 strips. Ignored when the pattern is "random" or "cross".

option.name: random_angle
option._long: Pick a fresh diagonal direction for the cut on every window close. Disable to always cut at the fixed angle below.

option.name: angle
option._long: Angle of the cut in degrees, measured from the horizontal. Only used when the random angle is disabled.

option.name: curve
option._long: How much the cuts bow, like a real sword stroke. 0 gives dead-straight cuts (which read more like a laser than a blade); higher values arc them more. Parallel cuts become concentric arcs and the trail follows the same curve.

option.name: spread
option._long: How far the pieces fly apart perpendicular to the cut. 1.0 sends them roughly a screen-width apart by the end.

option.name: trajectory
option._long: How the pieces fly off. "radial" shoots them straight out from the cut, accelerating. "ballistic" throws them out with an upward kick and lets gravity take over, so they rise, slow down and fall along a parabola like a real object.

option.name: lift
option._long: Ballistic trajectory only: how high the pieces are tossed before falling, as a fraction of the screen height (1.0 is about 15%). 0 throws them straight down with no hop.

option.name: gravity
option._long: How hard the pieces are pulled downward as they fly off. With the ballistic trajectory it scales how far past the bottom edge they land, so higher values feel heavier and fall faster.

option.name: fade
option._long: Gradually fade the flying pieces to invisible near the end. Disable to keep them fully opaque so they only disappear when they leave the screen (make sure gravity or spread carries them off-screen).

option.name: blade_enabled
option._long: Draw the glowing blade streak that sweeps across the window just before it splits.

option.name: blade_color
option._long: Color of the glowing sword trail. The alpha channel scales its brightness.

option.name: blade_width
option._long: Thickness of the bright core of the sword trail, in pixels. A softer glow extends beyond it.

option.name: sound_enabled
option._short: Play a sound on every cut
option._long: Play a sword-swish sound as each stroke lands, so multi-cut patterns are heard in sequence.

option.name: sound_file
option._long: Path to the audio file played on each cut (played with pw-play). Leave empty to disable.
